question: Frontend form for the operator UI v2: stay dependency-free single-file inline JS, or introduce a frontend build step (bundler + framework)?
author: claude/design

question: Trial detail default interaction: side panel preview with enter-to-full-page, or always navigate to the full page?
author: claude/design

question: Workspace discovery for bench serve --root: one-level directory scan for ledger.ndjson, or an explicit workspace registry file?
author: claude/design

question: Per-step content in trial drill-down: the wireframe shows message text and per-step edit patches, but TrajectoryStep captures only {kind, relative_ts, tokens, cost, files_touched, exit_code, command}. Drop the affordance, join native logs at presentation time, or add an additive content field to the versioned trajectory schema?
author: claude/design

rationale: Recommended option accepted via 2026-07-04 session review ('I accept your recommendation') after full nuance walkthrough. Basis: the content already exists post-redaction in the native logs (claude-code text blocks and edit-tool inputs are dropped at normalization today), and step content compounds the forensics mission (detectors and blinded advisory review see the moment of tampering, not just end-state), so the contract change pays beyond UI parity. Shape: schema_version 3 with additive nullable detail:str, kind-dependent semantics (message text / edit patch / output tail), empty-string=measured-empty vs null=unmeasured, exactly the command (v2, EVAL-11-D005) precedent; hash chain untouched (trial events carry only trajectory_sha; old records valid forever, no reader may require the field). Binding guardrails for the capture slice, to land as ACs when it graduates: (1) EVAL-12 redaction property tests extended with canaries planted inside detail; (2) forensics blinded-advisory-review scrub/identity-canary tests extended over detail; (3) dossier/timeline renderers exclude detail by default (self-containment and leak surface); (4) null-honest adapter asymmetry — claude-code fills text/patches, codex fills commands/output where measured, nulls never imputed, and the UI renders 'not captured in this record version' for absent detail; (5) no-reader-requires enforcement with the version bump. native-log-presentation-join rejected: unverified content beside sha-verified steps, adapter parsing smeared into presentation, no forensics gain. Sequencing: capture-side slice behind EVAL-14 P0; the UI ships current-schema rendering first and lights detail up when present. Per-step patch affordances are claude-code-mostly by data availability; wireframe annotated accordingly.
author: jyang
